events.title: Neurodivergent Family Night
events.description:
Join us for a free evening where children and caretakers are invited to play in a sensory-friendly, neuro-affirming environment! We encourage the whole family to attend, and therapists can join as well.
Neurodivergent Family Nights are held on the second Tuesday and fourth Thursday of each month from 5-7 pm (after we close to general admission).
On these nights, we dim the lights in the museum, turn off the sounds, and offer a limited capacity of 250 guests. Staff are happy and ready to assist with providing various materials, including noise-cancelling headphones, fidget toys, sunglasses, and weighted materials for your convenience.
RSVP HERE
*Registration is required and opens two weeks before each event. 
Play Partners:
Sterling Autism Institute: Sterling Autism is a private group practice with locations in Newport Beach and Long Beach, comprising psychologists, therapists, and psychiatrists. Their team is dedicated to providing collaborative, neurodiversity-affirming, evidence-based care and assessment for autistic children, teens, and adults. They work with neurodivergent children, adolescents, and adults. Conditions such as autism and ADHD are examples of neurodiversity. Identifying these differences can help people better understand themselves, strengthen communication and relationships, and access helpful support and services. 
Their services include diagnostic assessments, individual therapy for neurodivergent children and their siblings and parents, medication management, parent consultation and treatment planning, behavioral support and parent training, and groups. 
Philharmonic Society Music Mobile: Arriving in a Music Mobile van full of instruments, Philharmonic Society docents introduce the symphony orchestra and musical concepts with an engaging 30-minute presentation and instrument petting zoo, allowing students to try out different instruments in this hands-on experience. 
https://www.philharmonicsociety.org/education-community/education-programs/elementary/music-mobile

events.title: Little Hands Big Impact with Second Harvest
events.display_title: Little Hands Big Impact with Second Harvest
events.description:
Join us and Second Harvest Food Bank as we encourage little ones to be active members of society. This volunteer opportunity will encourage and open the platform for children to be able to package fruits that will be sent out to real families in need in Orange County.
TIMES: 10 am – 12 pm

Included in museum admission. Space is limited! Book your tickets now. 
*Members do not require a reservation.
*Events and dates are subject to change.

events.title: Meet the Instrument with Philharmonic Society
events.display_title: Meet the Instrument with Philharmonic Society
events.description:
Representatives from the Philharmonic Society will be visiting to give a performance and share some information on how strings, woodwinds, brass, and percussion create beautiful music. It’s a fun, informative way for kids (and also grown-ups) to learn about various instruments.
TIME: 11 AM Included in museum admission. Space is limited! Book your tickets now. 
*Members do not require a reservation.
*Events and dates are subject to change.

events.title: TeensTeach
events.description:
Learn, explore, and connect during this engaging program designed and led by local teens!  Through creative activities and hands-on opportunities, Teens Teach empowers young leaders to share their skills, spark curiosity, and inspire the next generation of learners. 
Join us between 1–2pm for an hour of connection, service, and community-led fun.

events.title: Toddler Town
events.description:
We’re opening the city just for toddlers! 
Climb, dance, problem-solve, and pretend your way through our miniature world, designed to support movement, connection, and big developmental leaps. 
Designed for children 19 months to 3 years. 
This program is exclusively for toddlers and their caregivers. No older siblings, please. 
*Registration is required, including members*
Every Monday!
9:00 AM – 10:30 AM
Fee: $12 per person  
GENERAL PUBLIC REGISTER HERE
MEMBERS REGISTER HERE

FAQs
Is Kidstock included with admission?Yes. All performances are included with regular museum admission.
What ages is Kidstock best for?The festival is designed for children ages 0–8 and their families.
Do performances happen every day?Yes. Live music or dance performances take place daily during the festival, except when the museum is closed.

events.title: Baby Steps
events.description:
Dedicated to children 18 months and younger, Pretend City hosts TWO Baby Steps programs a month on the first Tuesday and second Sunday. This program is a great way to socialize your little one and offers new learning opportunities in a safe space. This is perfect for first-time parents who can engage with one another, learn about other resources in the community, all while playing without older children in the museum. 
This event features a story time at 8:45 am in Orange Plaza and finishes off the morning in our grand exit parade. 
NO OLDER SIBLINGS PLEASE 

8-9:30 am 
$12 per adult (non-member) 
0-18 months are free 
Members are free 

RSVP HERE

events.title: Way to Play Day
events.description: Way to Play Days are held on the 2nd Friday of every month. This event allows parents and caregivers to complete free health screenings including developmental, dental, vision, and/or speech. Local child development experts are on site to provide information and assist grown-ups with questions they may have regarding their child’s development.

events.title: We Read Wednesdays with Empowering Readers OC
events.display_title: We Read Wednesdays with Empowering Readers OC
events.description:
Pretend City Children’s Museum and Parentis Foundation are partnering for something truly magical at We Read Wednesdays, where Littles and Grans read together! Our wonderful Empowering Readers OC reading mentors are bringing the joy of storytelling into the playful world of Pretend City, where imagination comes alive and every corner invites discovery.
This partnership is so important because it introduces children to the love of books in a way that feels fun, welcoming, and shared. When young learners and older adult mentors gather in a place built for wonder, stories become more than words on a page. They become moments of connection, confidence, and inspiration across generations.
Together, Pretend City and Empowering Readers OC are creating a community where reading is joyful and every story sparks a love of reading.

TIME: 11am – 12pm
About the Play Partner:
Empowering Readers OC, a program of Parentis Foundation, provides evidence-based literacy intervention and intergenerational mentoring to students in grades 1–5 who are reading below grade level. By pairing trained older adult volunteers with students, the program builds strong reading skills, confidence, and a foundation for lifelong learning.

events.title: Cooking in the City
events.description:
Join us in imaginative cooking with REAL tasty food from Lebanon.

Cooking Activities: 11 am & 2:30 pm

*This is not a cooking class; we will engage in imaginative play and have an opportunity to enjoy pre-made cooked food


About the play partner: 
Leah Khoury is a CPA by day and author by night (and lunch breaks and weekends). She loves ducks, traveling, and making people smile (in no particular order) and from these loves Benny on the Move was born! ✨🐣🌎✈️ 
www.BennyontheMove.com
Instagram: @BennyontheMove


Space is limited! Book your tickets in advance to avoid delays!
*Members do not require a reservation.
*Events and dates are subject to change.

events.title: Good to Go Fair
events.description:
Pretend City Children’s Museum Good To Go Fair: A FREE Family-Friendly Event! This FREE event is perfect for families with children ages 0-5. Enjoy a variety of activities, resources, and services designed to support your child’s development and overall well-being.
TIME: 9 am-1 pm
Pretend City Parking Lot
Rain or Shine

Developmental Screenings:
⦁ Check your child’s developmental milestones and receive valuable insights into their growth
⦁ Vision Screenings: Detect early signs of vision issues to ensure your child’s eyes are healthy.
⦁ Dental Screenings: Keep your child’s smile bright and healthy with a free dental check-up.
⦁ Social-Emotional Screenings: Assess your child’s emotional well-being and get support for their mental health.
⦁ And more!
Why Attend?
Early Detection: Catch potential health concerns early, allowing for timely intervention and support.
Free Services: Access these important health screenings at no cost to you.
Expert Guidance: Receive advice and resources from trained professionals to support your child’s development and health.
PLUS, your child can earn a FREE ticket to Pretend City by completing 3 Health screenings!
Don’t miss the chance to connect with incredible organizations!
Our Sponsors: CalOptima Health, Fairmont Schools, Hoag, Orange County Department of Education, PNC Bank
*The event is free; please note admission to the museum is not included.

events.title: Earth Day in the City
events.description:
Celebrate Earth Day at Pretend City with activities that help children learn how to care for our planet. Families are invited to explore simple, hands-on experiences that highlight nature, conservation, and everyday ways we can all help protect the Earth. 

TIMES: 11 AM & 2:30 PM

*Members do not require a reservation.
*Events and dates are subject to change.

About the play partner:

OC Waste & Recycling serves the County’s solid waste disposal needs by providing waste management services, operating public landfills, protecting the local environment, investing in renewable energy enterprises and promoting recycling in order to ensure a safe and healthy community for current and future generations. OCWR also provides education, outreach, and sustainability programs that help residents reduce waste and support state and local mandates.
